symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid tense joint swelling within an hour of trivial mechanical loading

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemarthrosis of severe Haemophilia A
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Speed and tenseness of the effusion after trivial trauma is characteristic of a factor-deficient joint bleed.
A ligament tear rarely produces such rapid, tense swelling from so trivial a load.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known severe Haemophilia A with recurrent prior joint bleeds

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemarthrosis of severe Haemophilia A
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tense atraumatic/low-trauma joint bleed in a person with confirmed severe FVIII deficiency and a bleed history is a haemarthrosis until proven otherwise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete inability to bear weight; joint locked in slight flexion

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemarthrosis of severe Haemophilia A
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A large tense effusion mechanically blocks and splints the joint in slight flexion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to access stored factor concentrate; alone and immobile

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemarthrosis of severe Haemophilia A
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Does not change the diagnosis but converts a home-manageable bleed into an emergency requiring in-person factor delivery — decisive for triage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile; no systemic infective features (home thermometer ~37C)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever lowers the probability of joint sepsis, though it cannot exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distal foot warm, pink, sensate and mobile (patient self-check)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute compartment syndrome of the leg
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Warm, perfused, sensate toes make established compartment syndrome unlikely at this moment; a self-check is not a substitute for clinician neurovascular assessment, so it remains a monitored red flag.

symptoms.symptom_name: No head strike, headache, vomiting or other bleeding site

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No head trauma and no neurological symptoms lower the immediate concern for intracranial bleed, which nonetheless must be safety-netted in any severe haemophiliac.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe pain unrelieved by oral paracetamol/codeine

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemarthrosis of severe Haemophilia A
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Capsular distension pain is typically severe and poorly responsive to simple oral analgesia; non-specific but consistent.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense warm shiny joint with exquisite tenderness and near-zero range of motion on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemarthrosis of severe Haemophilia A
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician palpation of a tense effusion and mechanical block confirms a large intra-articular collection — sealed, cannot be obtained by the AI in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vitals (BP 135/85, HR 110, RR 18, afebrile) and neurovascular exam

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemarthrosis of severe Haemophilia A
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia is consistent with severe pain; the measured values are clinician-obtained and sealed — the AI infers distress from the patient's report, not a measured pulse.

symptoms.symptom_name: Factor VIII activity level, full blood count and inhibitor screen

investigation_details.investigation_name: Factor VIII assay + FBC + inhibitor screen
investigation_details.expected_result: Very low/absent FVIII activity confirming severe deficiency; inhibitor screen guides whether standard factor will work
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot confirm factor level or detect an inhibitor that would render standard replacement ineffective — a hospital/HTC investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemarthrosis of severe Haemophilia A
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the deficiency and detects inhibitors; a sealed laboratory investigation the AI cannot obtain.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Classic acute haemarthrosis in severe haemophilia
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute haemarthrosis of severe Haemophilia A
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Known severe FVIII deficiency + rapid tense low-trauma joint swelling + mechanical block + severe pain unrelieved by simple analgesia is the recognisable joint-bleed constellation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person Emergency Department / Haemophilia Treatment Centre assessment for factor replacement
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive treatment (Factor VIII) and neurovascular/compartment assessment must happen in person and promptly; the AI arranges this rather than performing or dosing it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Factor VIII activity, FBC and inhibitor screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms deficiency severity and detects an inhibitor that would change treatment; performed at the ED/HTC, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration / imaging to exclude septic arthritis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Considered under factor cover if infection cannot be excluded clinically; a specialist decision, not a routine or AI-ordered step, and never performed without haemostatic cover.
